name: Kortny Product Benchmark
purpose: Keep Kortny engineering grounded in visible Slack coworker quality, latency and cost, context/tool correctness, and operator trust.
operating_rule: New slices should improve Slack response quality, latency/cost, correct context/tool selection, or operator trust/debuggability.

slack: Responds briefly and naturally.
Does not introduce itself as a separate runtime or agent.
Does not tag Kortny instead of the Slack user.
logs: No adk_planned_workflow_selected event.
No Composio catalog fanout for this prompt.
db: Task succeeds with one posted result message.
LLM usage has nonzero recorded cost when a model call is made.
dashboard: Task detail shows Posted Slack Response.
No Planned Trace panel is shown.

scenarios.title: Channel profile with provenance
scenarios.prompt: @Kortny what do you know about how this channel is used, and why do you believe that?

slack: Explains the channel purpose and recurring work patterns.
States what evidence was used and any sampling caveat.
Does not invent stored graph facts when graph retrieval returns none.
logs: Uses channel context, workspace graph, or Slack history where available.
Records kg_retrieval_completed or channel history tool evidence when used.
db: Task event timeline includes context assembly.
Any graph facts used are scope-safe for the channel.
dashboard: Timeline makes selected context and tools inspectable.
Posted response and raw result are both visible.

scenarios.title: Recent decisions and unresolved items
scenarios.prompt: @Kortny summarize the last few decisions in this channel and call out anything unresolved

slack: Separates decisions from unresolved items.
Avoids citing HIGs or project facts unless they are present in visible channel context.
Keeps answer concise enough for Slack.
logs: Uses Slack/channel context when needed.
Does not run broad web research for a local channel-summary request.
db: Task succeeds with source/tool trace if channel history is read.
Episode retrieval is scoped to thread, channel, or user.
dashboard: Timeline shows channel context or tool-call provenance.
Cost stays within quick-task expectations.

scenarios.title: Follow-up memory instruction
scenarios.prompt: Yeah, let's do that. Remember this preference for future reports.
scenarios.known_gap: Intent handling can classify the memory candidate correctly while the later task path still performs unrelated follow-up work.

slack: Acknowledges the memory candidate or asks one concise clarification.
Does not execute the previous analytical task again unless explicitly requested.
logs: Records memory candidate or memory confirmation flow.
Does not route to planned workflow solely because the phrase is a follow-up.
db: Creates or proposes one memory row only when the preference text is specific enough.
Does not create a duplicate analytical task from stale thread context.
dashboard: Memory event is visible in task timeline.
Any skipped memory explains why it was skipped.

scenarios.title: Scoped Linear project task summary
scenarios.prompt: @Kortny can you summarize my open tasks in the Linear kortny project?
scenarios.known_gap: Composio tool signatures can still cause weak Linear filtering or ask for details already present in the prompt.

slack: Uses the connected Linear integration when eligible.
Does not ask for the project name when 'kortny project' is already supplied.
Clearly states if it cannot confirm assignment or open status.
logs: Shows Linear Composio tool selection and execution.
Tool errors are recoverable and summarized rather than causing a generic failure.
db: Connection scope respects channel/workspace vs DM/user policy.
Task records tools_used with Linear-related tool names.
dashboard: Tool result payload is inspectable.
Any tool validation failure is visible with argument keys.

scenarios.title: Multi-source research plus workspace comparison
scenarios.prompt: @Kortny research AI observability tools, check Linear for existing HIGs, compare with our docs, and recommend next actions

slack: Leads with recommendation and separates evidence from blockers.
Does not leak internal monologue or ADK role names.
Mentions material source/tool limitations.
logs: Emits adk_planned_workflow_selected.
Records branch, tool, budget, and sanitizer events.
db: LLM usage cost is nonzero for every priced model call.
Planned workflow budget events appear if caps are reached.
dashboard: Planned Trace panel shows branches, budget controls, tool rollup, and raw-vs-posted response.
Timeline shows both raw planned output and final Slack-posted output.

scenarios.title: Deeper entertainment research synthesis
scenarios.prompt: Do a deeper pass on the top James Bond films using ratings, critic consensus, and cultural impact.
scenarios.known_gap: Current planned-workflow classifier treats this as inline even though the user asked for a deeper multi-source synthesis.

slack: Gives a ranked answer with a clear scoring lens.
Uses sources or states when relying on general knowledge.
Avoids excessive source dumping.
logs: Should enter planned workflow or a bounded research path.
Should avoid runaway repeated search calls.
db: Cost and token totals remain under the planned benchmark budget.
Tool calls stop at the configured total cap.
dashboard: Planned Trace or equivalent research trace shows why sources were enough.
Budget hits are visible if the task stops early.

scenarios.title: Website audit with named framework
scenarios.prompt: @Kortny review harrypotter.com and audit it with the CPT framework. Point out gaps and suggested reframe opportunities.
scenarios.known_gap: Current planned-workflow classifier misses website audit plus named-framework requests as complex planned work.

slack: Crawls or searches the site before making claims.
Uses the CPT framework explicitly and produces concrete reframes.
Formats a detailed but readable Slack answer.
logs: Uses web/search/crawl integration when available.
Records tool evidence and any pages that could not be accessed.
db: Source refs or tool output include inspected site pages.
No generic answer is recorded without external evidence.
dashboard: Trace shows site inspection path and final synthesis.
Operator can see limitations if crawl/search failed.

scenarios.title: Forget memory with no matching fact
scenarios.known_gap: Current planned-workflow classifier can treat forget-memory requests as planned due keyword noise from 'PDF'.

slack: If no matching memory exists, explains that naturally.
Does not generic-fail when the forget tool returns no match.
Does not claim deletion unless a fact was actually forgotten.
logs: Uses forget_fact or inspect_memory only as needed.
No planned workflow event for a simple memory mutation.
db: If a matching fact exists, status changes preserve audit history.
If no match exists, no unrelated memory rows are changed.
dashboard: Timeline shows tool result and humanized final response.
Memory page reflects unchanged or forgotten state correctly.

scenarios.title: Document iteration with current data
scenarios.prompt: @Kortny enhance the PYPL report with more company info, current price info, earnings call info, and generate a 3-page PDF.
scenarios.known_gap: Document iterations still need stronger artifact versioning and proof that the new file materially differs from the prior file.

slack: States what changed from the prior report.
Posts one versioned PDF artifact.
Does not rename the internal document title without reason.
logs: Reads the attached/prior PDF when available.
Uses current-data tools for price and earnings evidence.
Records artifact creation and Slack file posting.
db: Artifact row has stable filename lineage or version suffix.
Task source refs include the prior file and current-data source.
dashboard: Artifacts table shows generated PDF.
Trace shows file-read, research/current-data, and PDF-generation steps.
